title: Print setup in Gantt views

posts.author: leticiaggarcia (Letícia Goulart Garcia)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello! We’re having some feedbacks about the print section on monday.com
I understand and already like the real-time print screen… but now we need to present a professional report on PDF.
Things that need to be improved:

  • Gantt date range ( all dates or custom date range " start to end " )
  • Paper size ( A4, A3… many gantt charts have long timelines… the scale for A4 papers don’t fit nicely)
  • Scaling ( fit to width or scale “__” % )

And more options:

  • include company logo
  • include board name
  • include date/time stamp
  • include page number
  • include comments
  • include collapsed rows

Thank you so much, We can do this!!!

posts.author: jameswright (james)
posts.cookedHtml: When will this be fixed. Looks like its been 2 years with no movement.

posts.author: Hanael (Hanael)
posts.cookedHtml:
Same!

Paper and prints are still alive. , Gantt chart are key in the building industry, in architecture/urban planning, in all sorts of long term planning to name the few.

Here Monday shows a weak point. PDF export is barely usable. Mainly because it’s not possible to select/ajust a time frame to export (it’s all or nothing), and as soon as the timeline goes longer than 2 years, pdf export is not possible. Not to mention no page formating capabilities.

XLS export is… well… not even close.

Use cases where even a digital animal uses good old fashion paper:

  • Hard copy to be distributed/pinned on site
  • Hard copy instead of your expensive 50’’ retina screen in hard conditions such as dusty building sites.
  • A pdf proving the project planning at a given time (attached to the minutes, to cover legal risks, etc.).
  • A working base for drafting and adjusting things on it. We all like to doodle, because it’s more efficient.

posts.author: GanttGo (Hidai Bar-Mor)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @leticiaggarcia , are you still running into this? I built a monday marketplace app called GanttGo that covers most of the requirements mentioned above- PDF export with multiple papers sizes, multi-page printing, company logo, board name as well as export to excel and power point. Happy to answer any question!
